query: What is the composition and role of the Senate as per statutory guidelines?
retrieved_context:
[Reference 1 | Source: IITJ_Rules_Regulations_Structured.pdf | Page: 2]
The academic programmes are based on three tenets: (1) Semester System, (2) Credit System, and (3) Relative
Grading. These are administered by a duly constituted Academic Committee (AC) chaired by Dean (Academics).
The Senate formulates guidelines and rules for high standards and smooth functioning. Programmes can include
additional requirements subject to Senate approval.
2. ACADEMIC SESSION
The academic session runs from end of July in one year to mid-July of the next year, divided into:
[
  {
    "Term": "Semester I",
    "Period": "Fourth week of July to last week of November"
  },
  {
    "Term": "Semester II",
    "Period": "Last week of December to last week of April"
  },
  {
    "Term": "Summer Term",
    "Period": "Middle of May to middle of July"
  }
]
Excluding examination days, total instruction days per semester: at least 70.
2.1 Academic Calendar
The academic calendar specifies dates for orientation, registration, commencement of classes, add/drop of courses,
examinations, grade submission, project evaluation, results declaration, mid-semester recess, and vacation. Approved
by the Senate.
3. REGISTRATION

---

17.4 No Disciplinary Action: Student with any pending indiscipline case not considered to have completed
requirements.
17.5 Award of Degrees: Provisional Certificate issued on Chairman Senate approval. Degree Certificate requires
Senate recommendation to Board of Governors and BoG approval in ensuing convocation.
17.6 Withdrawal of Degree: Senate may recommend BoG to withdraw a degree in case of gross violation of
graduation requirements detected at a later stage.
18. AMENDMENTS: The Senate of IIT Jodhpur reserves the right to modify/amend, without notice, Rules and
Regulations of the Postgraduate programmes.
 STATUTORY BODIES AND KEY FUNCTIONARIES

answer:
1.  The Senate of IIT Jodhpur is a governing body responsible for overseeing the postgraduate programs at the institution.
2.  It is composed of the Chairman, Board of Governors, Board of Governors (BoG), Senate, Institute, Dean of Faculty Affairs (DOFA), Associate Dean, Faculty Affairs (ADSA), Dean of Students Affairs (DOSA), and other key personnel.
3.  The Senate’s role includes amending rules and regulations, approving degrees, and reviewing disciplinary actions.
4.  The Senate also establishes the Governance Structure, which encompasses the IIT Council, IIT Governance Structure (ASCII Tree), and various committees.
5.  The Senate's authority is defined by statutory bodies and key personnel, including the IIT Council, Board of Governors, and other functional committees.

The governance hierarchy of IIT Jodhpur is structured as follows:

■■■ Director
■ ■■■ Senate
■ ■■■ Institute
■ ■■■ Buildings & Works Committee

answer:
Here's the answer to your question:

The B.Tech. Electrical Engineering program requires a total of 120 credits.

Here are some core courses:

*   Data Structures and Algorithms
*   Circuit Theory
*   Engineering Electromagnetics
*   Physical Electronics
*   Power Engineering
*   Smart Grid
*   Electrical Machines (PC), Electrical Machines Lab (PC)
*   Electrical Machines
*   Power Engineering
*   Measurement and Instrumentation (PE), Power System Analysis and Stability (PE), Power System Operation and Control (PE), Power Electronics (PE), Industrial Drives (PE)

Performance evaluated continuously via classroom interaction, examinations, lab work, term-papers, and projects.

attempt unsatisfactory.

query: What details are provided about examination scheduling and timetabling?
retrieved_context:
[Reference 1 | Source: IITJ_Rules_Regulations_Structured.pdf | Page: 2]
examinations, grade submission, project evaluation, results declaration, mid-semester recess, and vacation. Approved
by the Senate.
3. REGISTRATION
a. Registration: Students must register for prescribed credits on dates specified in the Academic Calendar. Late
registration requires a fine.
b. Provisional Registration: Fresh students awaiting final qualifying exam results may register provisionally on
submission of a certificate from the last institution. Documents must be submitted by the last date in the Academic
Calendar.
c. Late Registration: Failure to register by the late registration date results in deregistration. Appeals go to Chairman,
Senate through Dean Academics.
d. Adding and Dropping of Courses: Students may add or drop course(s) with Faculty Advisor approval till the last date
of registration.
e. Withdrawing from a Course: Withdrawal is permitted until typically within four weeks from semester start, as per
academic calendar. Withdrawn course appears on grade card with grade 'W'.
f. Cancellation of Semester Registration: Absence for 4+ weeks during a semester causes automatic cancellation of
registration from all courses.

---

[Reference 2 | Source: IITJ_Rules_Regulations_Structured.pdf | Page: 6]
papers. Instructor must keep evaluation records for at least 6 months. Courses may be conducted through: (i) direct
contact, (ii) synchronized remote contact, or (iii) guided study mode, or combination. Formal evaluations are always
under direct physical supervision.
4.1.7 Teaching Assignments: Allocated by Head of Department hosting the Programme.
4.1.8 Auditing of Courses: Permitted with Dean (Academics) approval on instructor and Faculty Advisor
recommendation. Listed on Grade Card.
4.1.9 Attendance Requirements: Full attendance expected. Student allowed to appear for exams even below 75%.
Students below 75% must score at least C grade to pass; otherwise awarded F grade.
4.2 Evaluation
Performance evaluated continuously via classroom interaction, examinations, lab work, term-papers, and projects.

CIRCULAR: Amendment in Attendance Policy — No.
IITJ/OAA/Circular/2024-25/51/68.2 — Dated: 08 July 2024
The Senate in its 38th Meeting (26th June 2024, resolution 38.2.7) approved an amendment to the existing attendance
policy.
Existing Policy: Student must have full attendance. Even below 75%, student allowed to appear for exams. Students
below 75% must score at least C grade; otherwise awarded F grade.
Revised Policy (supersedes earlier policy in totality):
Students expected to have full attendance unless leave is for valid medical or bonafide reasons. Attendance must not
fall below 75%. Attendance records made available weekly by instructors/TAs. Examination performance is NO
LONGER linked to course attendance.

---

Grade Descriptions:
A*: Same grade points as A. Awarded only in courses with 50+ registered students. Must not exceed 2% of strength.
Not awarded for projects/seminars.
E: Awarded when marks are below D cutoff but student has met 75% attendance. Student eligible for one additional
examination (within first week of next semester) to convert E to D.
I: Incomplete — awarded on student request due to absence on medical/special grounds. Must be resolved by last
date of add/drop of next semester.

■ Statutory Bodies and Key Functionaries
Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur
1. IIT Jodhpur — Statutory Governance Structure
The governance hierarchy of IIT Jodhpur is structured as follows:
IIT Council
■■■ Chairman, Board of Governors
■■■ Board of Governors (BoG)
■■■ Director
■ ■■■ Senate
■ ■■■ Institute
■ ■■■ Buildings & Works Committee
■■■ Finance Committee
Revised Administrative Structure — Deans & PICs
Each Dean oversees a portfolio with Associate Deans and Professors In-Charge (PICs):
Director
■■■ Dean of Faculty Affairs (DOFA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Faculty Affairs (ADFA)
■■■ Dean of Students Affairs (DOSA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Student Affairs (ADSA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
■ PICs: Training & Placement, Sports
■■■ Dean of Academic Affairs (DOAA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Academic Affairs (ADAA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Executive Education (ADEE)
■ PICs: Center for Continuing Education, Vocational Training & Upskilling, NIT Cell
■■■ Dean of International Relations (DOIR)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, International Connect (ADIC)
■■■ Dean of R&D; (DORD)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, R&D; (ADRD)
